title: Sparse3DPR: Training-Free 3D Hierarchical Scene Parsing and Task-Adaptive Subgraph Reasoning from Sparse RGB Views
abstract: The paper proposes Sparse3DPR, a training-free framework for open-ended scene understanding using pre-trained LLMs with sparse-view RGB inputs. It introduces a hierarchical plane-enhanced scene graph that supports open vocabulary, using planar structures as spatial anchors for clearer reasoning chains. A task-adaptive subgraph extraction method dynamically filters irrelevant information, reducing noise and improving efficiency. Results show a 28.7% EM@1 improvement and a 78.2% speedup compared with ConceptGraphs on the Space3D-Bench, with comparable performance to training-based methods on ScanQA and real-world experiments confirming robustness and generalization.
note: Published at AAAI 2026. Conference page: https://ojs.aaai.org/index.php/AAAI/article/view/37393. PDF also available at: https://ojs.aaai.org/index.php/AAAI/article/view/37393/41355
affiliations: State Key Laboratory of Multimodal Artificial Intelligence Systems, Institute of Automation, Chinese Academy of Sciences
School of Artificial Intelligence, University of Chinese Academy of Sciences
